content.full_text:
March 6, 1991

Sandra Taylor  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804-0814

Dear Ms. Taylor:

This letter will confirm the advice you provided me in telephone conversations held on January 31, 1991 and February 25, 1991. In those conversations, you advised that a committee which is controlled by a person who holds office in a locality which has a local election law imposing valid contribution limits may raise and spend unlimited amounts for local ballot measure activities, and additionally raise and spend funds within the local contribution limits for candidate activities.

Further, you advised that such a committee need not establish a separate bank account for the ballot measure activities so long as the committee engages exclusively in the ballot measure activities during an election cycle or other time period such that there is no possibility that any of the funds raised during that period would be used for candidate activities. In contrast, should such a committee during a particular period of time desire to raise and spend both unlimited funds for ballot measure activities and limited funds for candidate activities, the committee would need to establish a separate bank account for the ballot measure activities to ensure that the unlimited contributions were not used for candidate activities.

Should the above not comport with our conversations or otherwise the FPPC's position on these matters, please let me know immediately.

Sincerely yours,

[signature]
Philip R. Recht  
Manatt, Phelps & Phillips
